frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Arre, Geeta ki bahu toh kitni khush hai doosre bachhe se. Ghar mein roshni ho jaati hai. Par yeh Priya ko dekho, career ke peeche bhaag rahi hai. Yeh asli khushi aur sukh toh bachhon se hi milta hai na, kaam toh hamesha rahega.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Sunita implies that Priya is misguided in pursuing a career over children, suggesting that 'true happiness' for a woman comes primarily from childbearing. This subtly frames career ambition as a lesser pursuit compared to a woman's natural role of having and raising children, aligning with the core belief that childbearing is a woman's primary duty and source of fulfillment. The 'kaam toh hamesha rahega' (work will always be there) reinforces the idea that career can wait, unlike childbearing.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 44. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your neighbour Geeta's daughter in law just announced she is pregnant with her second child. Geeta is overjoyed. You are sitting with Geeta and two other neighbourhood women having tea. The conversation turns to another woman in the colony Ñ Priya Ñ who has been married six years, has no children, and works at a private company. You are giving your opinion on Priya's situation and what you think her priorities are and what a woman who chooses career over children is really choosing.
